title: NVR Q2 Earnings Beat, Homebuilding Revenues Miss, Margin Down
text: NVR's second-quarter results reflect low housing activity and macroeconomic woes.

title: Skeptics In Pulte Homes Next Bull Run May Get Burnt
text: The residential construction sector has seen a stratospheric rise during the past year and a half, with leading dynamics in the underlying economy fueling further demand for housing and further amplifying the need for construction. PulteGroup NYSE: PHM is one of the lucky names that rode the rally wave with still more upside potential than meets the eye.

title: NVR, INC. ANNOUNCES SECOND QUARTER RESULTS
text: RESTON, Va. , July 25, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- NVR, Inc. (NYSE: NVR), one of the nation's largest homebuilding and mortgage banking companies, announced net income for its second quarter ended June 30, 2023 of $404.0 million, or $116.54 per diluted share.

title: Stock Split Watch: 3 Supercharged Growth Stocks That Could Split Their Shares in 2023
text: Stock splits have experienced a renaissance in popularity over the past few years, even continuing during the downturn. Many stocks that are investor favorites have enacted stock splits, making them more affordable for the average investor.

title: NVR Gears Up to Report Q2 Earnings: Here's What to Expect
text: NVR's second-quarter 2023 results are likely to be affected by high mortgage rates and ongoing economic uncertainties, resulting in declining housing demand.

title: Will a Decrease in Housing Starts Hurt Homebuilder Stocks?
text: Homebuilders traded lower as a group on July 19, ending the session down 0.20% after the National Association of Homebuilders reported that June housing starts fell more than expected.

title: LEN or NVR: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?
text: Investors interested in stocks from the Building Products - Home Builders sector have probably already heard of Lennar (LEN) and NVR (NVR). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now?

title: Housing Demand Gives Home Builders a Solid Foundation for More Gains
text: Home-builder stocks D.R. Horton, Lennar, and NVR are up between 29% and 33% this year, well above the S&P 500's 13% gain.
